title: cereus neomexicanus
intro: Cereus neomexicanus, also known as the New Mexico columnar cactus, is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Cereus neomexicanus include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and crushing the stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a poultice.
